page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 512 GB of dedicated system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage options.

In benchmark evaluations, the n2-standard-128 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. While single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests falls within the average range, multi-core execution is highly competitive, placing in the top 25 percent. The system excels in cryptographic operations, extended instruction execution, and ray tracing, achieving top-tier performance in the top 10 percent of benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is also robust, with uncached read speeds reaching 309.76 GB/sec and write speeds at 135.09 GB/sec.

This hardware profile is well-suited for highly parallelized workloads such as large-scale web serving, database operations, and software compilation. It handles Redis operations and static web serving efficiently, registering strong throughput metrics. However, for large-scale language model text generation, such as 70B parameter models, performance drops to the bottom 25 percent, indicating a clear tradeoff when attempting heavy AI inference without dedicated GPU acceleration. It represents a resource-dense, cost-efficient option for enterprise-grade general-purpose computing where massive CPU concurrency and balanced memory are required.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is a general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture with 64 physical cores. It features a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmarks show average single-core performance but strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25 percent for multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and database operations. It achieves top-tier results in cryptography and ray tracing. While highly efficient for concurrent workloads like static web serving and Redis databases, it exhibits limitations in large-scale LLM text generation due to the absence of hardware acceleration. This instance is best suited for enterprise-scale parallel processing, web serving, and database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is a general-purpose server with 128 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic execution for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 features 128 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for multi-core enterprise workloads and database operations.
tagline: A high-concurrency x86_64 compute platform featuring one hundred twenty-eight virtual cores and balanced memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture.
Strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in the top 25 percent.
Top-tier execution speeds for cryptographic and ray tracing workloads.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Optimal for large-scale web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
